biography:
脙聣douard Lucas was educated at the 脙聣cole Normale in Amiens. After this he worked at the Paris Observatory under Le Verrier .
During the Franco-Prussian War (1870 - 1871) Lucas served as an artillery officer. After the French were defeated, Lucas became professor of mathematics at the Lyc脙漏e Saint Louis in Paris. He later became professor of mathematics at the Lyc脙漏e Charlemagne, also in Paris.
Lucas is best known for his results in number theory : in particular he studied the Fibonacci sequence and the associated Lucas sequence is named after him. He gave the well-known formula for the Fibonacci numbers 5 f n = ( 1 + 5 2 ) n 芒聢聮 ( 1 芒聢聮 5 2 ) n \sqrt 5 f_{n} = \left(\Large\frac{1 + \sqrt 5}{2}\right)^{n}\normalsize - \left(\Large\frac{1 - \sqrt 5}{2}\right)^{n} 5 芒聙聥 f n 芒聙聥 = ( 2 1 + 5 芒聙聥 芒聙聥 ) n 芒聢聮 ( 2 1 芒聢聮 5 芒聙聥 芒聙聥 ) n . Lucas also devised methods of testing primality, essentially those used today. In 1876 he used his methods to prove that the Mersenne number 2 127 芒聢聮 1 2^{127} - 1 2 1 2 7 芒聢聮 1 is prime . This remains the largest prime number discovered without the aid of a computer.
The Lucas test for primes was refined by Lehmer in 1930 . It works as follows. Define the sequence S 2 = 4 , S 3 = 14 , S 4 = 194 , . . . S_{2} = 4, S_{3} = 14, S_{4} = 194, . . . S 2 芒聙聥 = 4 , S 3 芒聙聥 = 1 4 , S 4 芒聙聥 = 1 9 4 , . . . where for n > 2 , S n n > 2, S_{n} n > 2 , S n 芒聙聥 is defined inductively by S n = S n 芒聢聮 1 2 芒聢聮 2 S_{n} = S_{n-1}^{2} - 2 S n 芒聙聥 = S n 芒聢聮 1 2 芒聙聥 芒聢聮 2 . The Lucas- Lehmer test states that a Mersenne number M p = 2 p 芒聢聮 1 M_{p} = 2^{p} - 1 M p 芒聙聥 = 2 p 芒聢聮 1 , with p > 2 p > 2 p > 2 , is prime if and only if M p M_{p} M p 芒聙聥 divides S p S_{p} S p 芒聙聥 .
Lucas showed that S 127 S_{127} S 1 2 7 芒聙聥 is divisible by M 127 M_{127} M 1 2 7 芒聙聥 thus showing that M 127 M_{127} M 1 2 7 芒聙聥 is prime. This was a extremely difficult calculation since M 127 M_{127} M 1 2 7 芒聙聥 is a big number and S 127 S_{127} S 1 2 7 芒聙聥 is unbelievably large. In fact M 127 M_{127} M 1 2 7 芒聙聥 = 170141183460469231731687303715884105727 and Lucas was only able to perform the calculation since he showed that S 127 S_{127} S 1 2 7 芒聙聥 is divisible by M 127 M_{127} M 1 2 7 芒聙聥 without calculating S 127 S_{127} S 1 2 7 芒聙聥 .
Lucas is also well known for his invention of the Tower of Hanoi puzzle and other mathematical recreations. The Tower of Hanoi puzzle appeared in 1883 under the name of M. Claus. Notice that Claus is an anagram of Lucas!

His four volume work on recreational mathematics R脙漏cr脙漏ations math脙漏matiques 芒聯聣 ( Mathematical recreations ) (1882 - 94) has become a classic.
Lucas died as the result of a freak accident at a banquet when a plate was dropped and a piece flew up and cut his cheek. He died of erysipelas a few days later.
